images.name: bike_art_base.jpg
images.description: bicycle art photo, 2.3MB, 2559x1439, rembg mask coverage=28.4%

images.name: bench_rgb.jpg
images.description: outdoor bench photo, 7.0MB, 3150x4200, rembg mask coverage=59.0%

summary: rembg was operational (v2.0.75) for all 3 images. Mode C flicker_std PASS is NOT consistent across n=3. Only bike_art_base.jpg (28.4% fg coverage, compact subject) achieves std=0.0086 <= 0.01. bench_rgb.jpg (59.0% fg, tall vertical subject) and cliffhanger_base.png (34.1% fg, landscape) both fail at 0.0218 and 0.0190 respectively. Bootstrap 95% CI [0.0086, 0.0218] crosses the 0.01 threshold; the claim does not generalize. fg_entropy passes on all 3 images (>=3.0), confirming that image complexity is not the bottleneck. The flicker failure is caused by large fg coverage + deep parallax warp producing visible jitter in Mode C. v0.1 requires pixel-level hole fill and/or warp smoothing to generalize flicker_std <= 0.01.
conclusion: C-mode alone insufficient for generalization. v0.1 must address: (1) pixel-level hole fill for high-coverage fg masks, (2) warp amplitude scaling by fg_coverage to limit jitter.
